channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Strawberry Moon to dazzle the night sky starting tonight as first full moon of the summer - FOX Weather
item.description:
1. Strawberry Moon to dazzle the night sky starting tonight as first full moon of the summer  FOX Weather

  3. June's Strawberry Moon is unlike any other full moon. Here's why  Space
  4. This Week's Sky at a Glance, June 26 – July 5  Sky & Telescope
  5. Monday night’s Strawberry Moon will play a trick on your eyes  WSMV

item.title: NASA to launch rescue mission June 30 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere - Space
item.description:
1. NASA to launch rescue mission June 30 to save Swift space telescope from burning up in Earth's atmosphere  Space

  3. NASA prepares for daring rescue to save aging telescope from falling to Earth  Yahoo
  4. Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission  NASA Science (.gov)
  5. Inside NASA’s daring race to save a doomed space telescope  USA Today

item.title: A rare dinosaur fossil from Antarctica is found tucked away in a drawer - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description:
1. A rare dinosaur fossil from Antarctica is found tucked away in a drawer  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  2. First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer  BBC
  3. First ever dinosaur fossil discovered on Antarctica identified as a titanosaur  Natural History Museum
  4. Scientists Stumble on Historic Discovery in Forgotten Drawer  The Daily Beast
  5. First ever dinosaur found in Antarctica described for science  Phys.org
item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: This Famously Weird 'Pink Planet' May Have Salty Clouds, Study Finds - ScienceAlert

item.title: Comparative primate analysis shows that humans are not unique in having a tight cephalopelvic fit at birth - Nature
item.description:
1. Comparative primate analysis shows that humans are not unique in having a tight cephalopelvic fit at birth  Nature
  2. A Long-Standing Theory of Childbirth Is a Myth  The Atlantic
  3. Childbirth is not uniquely difficult to humans  Phys.org
  4. Childbirth for many primate species is even harder than for humans  New Scientist
  5. Human Childbirth May Not Be Uniquely Difficult, as Some Squirrel Monkey Babies Have Heads Nearly Twice the Size of Their Mothers' Pelvic Space  Discover Magazine

item.title: Physicists create a strange new quantum state called a fractional fermi sea - ScienceDaily

  4. Physicists Set Sail on a Strange Quantum State Called a Fractional Fermi Sea  The Quantum Insider
  5. "It Has a Hidden Order That Becomes Visible”: Physicists Just Discovered a Bizarre New Quantum State  The Debrief

item.title: In July 2025, the ATLAS sky survey in Chile spotted a faint comet racing through the solar system at roughly 130,000 miles per hour — and follow-up orbit calculations quickly showed it was not bound to the Sun at all, but had arrived from interstellar space. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In July 2025, the ATLAS sky survey in Chile spotted a faint comet racing through the solar system at roughly 130,000 miles per hour — and follow-up orbit calculations quickly showed it was not bound to the Sun at all, but had arrived from interstellar space.  Space Daily
  2. 12 billion years old, this interstellar comet is older than our solar system  Phys.org
  3. California scientists scanned 3I/ATLAS for alien tech, and found only Earth-based noise  Yahoo
  4. Deuterium in Comets Tells Interesting Tales  Universe Today
  5. Interstellar comet that zoomed past Earth could be oldest and coldest object ever seen in solar system, astronomers say  CBS News

item.title: Two ‘Super-Puff’ Planets Lighter Than Candy Candy Discovered by Researchers - Good News Network
item.description:
1. Two ‘Super-Puff’ Planets Lighter Than Candy Candy Discovered by Researchers  Good News Network
  2. NASA’s TESS Mission Reveals the “Puffiest” Planets Ever Found  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Astronomers find 2 giant planets that are lighter than cotton candy  Action News 5
  4. These planets are 1,000 times bigger than Earth, but lighter than cotton candy: study  CBC
  5. Two Planets Lighter Than Candy Floss  Universe Today

item.title: The James Webb telescope picked out 16.5 million individual stars in the Cigar Galaxy — a neighbor forming stars ten times faster than the Milky Way, in a burst estimated to last only a few hundred million years - Space Daily
item.description:
1. The James Webb telescope picked out 16.5 million individual stars in the Cigar Galaxy — a neighbor forming stars ten times faster than the Milky Way, in a burst estimated to last only a few hundred million years  Space Daily
  2. NASA’s Webb Pinpoints Millions of Stars Within Cigar Galaxy  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. James Webb Space Telescope Captures the Cigar Galaxy’s Brilliant Stellar Halo in Pristine Detail  Nautilus | Science
  4. In a New 223-Megapixel Image, NASA Captures 16.5 Million Stars of the Cigar Galaxy  thisiscolossal.com
  5. The Galaxy Living Too Fast  Universe Today

item.description:

item.title: The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw - Space
item.description:
1. The James Webb Space Telescope peered into one of the universe's oldest galaxy clusters, and scientists can't explain what they saw  Space
  2. The JWST Spies Six Galaxies Becoming One  Universe Today
  3. The James Webb Space Telescope found a spiral galaxy, nicknamed the Big Wheel, that existed just two billion years after the Big Bang — five times more massive than the Milky Way, with a spiral structure our models say couldn't have survived that early  Space Daily
  4. Webb Telescope Finds Giant Galaxy, Black Hole Birth  Mirage News
  5. James Webb Space Telescope Spots Six Galaxies Merging Into One 12 Billion Years Ago  Tech Times

item.title: Sister Mary, a Catholic nun who belonged to the School Sisters of Notre Dame and died at 101 with her memory and reasoning fully intact, was one of 678 nuns enrolled in a 30-year study of brain aging by the American researcher David Snowdon — and her - Space Daily
